title: The Microphone lost quality super fast and then the Left Band completely falls apart
text:
Bought this to replace a gaming headset I was using which used the green and pink cables for audio/microphone. Surprisingly for about half the price this outperforms in sound quality and there is less cables around my desktop now which is a plus, and the controls have worked on my ASUS C202SA chromebook as well which was a nice touch. The plastic headband could be a bit sturdier but for the sound quality and the actual ear pieces themselves being super comfortable and not making my head sweat like most gaming headsets, it gets a pass for that. I was also told on numerous gaming sessions I sounded crystal clear and I could hear them crystal clear just as well. If you are looking for a budget Headset to step your game up, this is a great headset.

EDIT
was trying to adjust the headban and the thing completely comes off, like the side with the microphone, that just slid out all the way and i can see the cable connecting it,
Guess it was too good to be true
The mic

title: Super-sensitive hang-up button!
text: Great sound, but a ridiculously sensitive hangup button on the cord ruins it. By moving around, you will keep hanging up on people unintentionally!

title: Done.  I cut the cord. Literally
text: After 3 months and 10 days of this product's nonsense, I lost my temper, decided to stop screwing with it and took the scissors to the cord.  I'd used their H110 for years, 2 or 3 of them, each lasting 1 or 2 years or more (if I didn't drag it with my foot when I left my desk) which was great for a simple $10 headset.  The 100 had lightweight earphones.  Dual plugs.  Mono - fine for talking.  Rotating mic and I put it on the right side.  But they stopped selling it, so I bought this one.  Ugh.  Mic only goes on left.  Earphone pads are heavy and it's tight, tight, tight on my head. Uncomfortable.  Constantly screwing with the cord in my way on my lap because  computer is on my right. Half the time it worked. Half the time it didn't.  Either people couldn't hear me or else I couldn't hear them through the headset - only through my speakers.  Sometimes it would switch from one to the other from and work in Google Meets, then wouldn't 5 minutes later on Zoom.  It's routine for me to clean

title: comfortable but microphone doesn't adjust
text: I'm using this product right now to type this review, and I think it is very comfortable and will probably last longer than the other headset I had.  The microphone position seems difficult to get close enough to the corner of my mouth and that is  essential for word recognition.  I think I figured out a way now to make this adjustment by lowering the whole thing.  Now hopefully things should be better.  Maybe the product could have a more flexible microphone, so you could adjust it no matter where are you placed the headset on your head

title: Awesome
text: My husband asked for these headphones. Since working from home during this coronavirus he needed headphones for meetings with a mic. Sound quality is great. The stereo sound for music is also great. Headphones are adjustable and comfortable. Mic is easy to use flips up when not using it. Like the mute button on the cord for easy use.

title: Good sound but too small
text:
I was wearing gaming headphone for work in conferences and felt silly. These looked more professional so I figured I would give them a try.

The sound quality is pretty decent for the price, but after hours of wearing them, they start to hurt my ears because they are too tight. The control button also gets hit accidently quite often and that's annoying. If you have a small head and don't mind clipping the control button to your clothes, then I suppose these are a great choice, but for me, I will see if I think I need another pair.

title: Fits my Work Needs
text: I am currently using this on a work laptop for my many online meetings, primarily though WebEx but also Skype for Business and GoToMeeting. My co-workers tell me my voice is clear, I can hear theirs very well, and the mute and volume buttons on the line are very useful. I find the on the ear design a little tight for me, and I would really say it's comfortable nor really uncomfortable. The padding really is okay for an on the ear style. It is definitely a set I take over pretty quickly. In comparison I use a Bose set for my phone, and a wireless set for my desktop for gaming. This was really just something I wanted to attach to the work machine all the time and readily available to just hook up.  For the price, it's actually pretty good for what I use it for... I just wouldn't ever choose something like this to wear for hours. However if a family member wanted something for under twenty and to put on for no more than an hour... this is a good start until they decide to upgrade.  This i

title: People can't hear all the noise around me
text: This really works well for Teams calls. The microphone and the noise cancelling in MS Teams seem to work very well, where I have been able to attend meetings and am heard very clearly without too much background noise.

title: Buy them
text: [[VIDEOID:537c7accd557ef46fc23e9e3beec9b05]] I’m very satisfied with this purchase. In the past I’ve had issue with the earphones being uncomfortable, however  the cushions on these are sooo comfortable, barely know that they’re on. The design is sleek sound is crisp BEST earphones I’ve EVER had.

title: Works great but hurts my ears after a couple hours
text:
I got this headset to practice working with speech recognition software such as Dragon NaturallySpeaking. Overall, I've been surprised and incredibly impressed with the performance. The recognition is better than with the standard headset that came with the software, and the headset does everything I had hoped.

There's a volume control button on the cord, and the USB cord is about 8 feet long, which is plenty generous for connecting to my computer and doing speech recognition.

The only problem with this headset is that I want to use it for hours on end while narrating articles. I'm a freelance writer, and sometimes I either just don't feel like typing, have a sore wrist or fingers, or feel like I can narrate my thoughts faster than I can type them. After about two hours of using this headset, my ears start to ache. I haven't figured out any way of changing the tension on this headset so it doesn't press so hard on my ears.

While I would definitely recom

title: Addendum: Headband Broke, Weak USB Connection --
text: The usb connecter does not seem to stand up to repeated insertion and removal.

title: Very nice as you can mute yourself if you want to ...
text: Use these for gaming and to chat during dungeons/raids. Very nice as you can mute yourself if you want to just listen in. Crystal clear sound. I also use them for listening to movies and music if my husband is sleeping and I don't want to wake him. I love them. One thing I noticed though is after having them on for about 3 or 4 hours, my head feels like it's being "squished" and I have marks on my ears. No big deal to me, but others may not like that.

title: 2 functions I needed didn't work
text: I am looking for headphones with microphone because I work from home and do many video conference calls and record trainings.  I have been looking for microphone that blocks surrounding sound and this set claimed to do this.  I immediately tested and it did not successfully block any of the surrounding sound; it picked up someone walking by in socks on hardwood floor. Also, as another reviewer noted, the mute button didn't work at all.  Being able to mute is key as well when working from remote locations.  I was very disappointed because other reviews seemed to indicate these would work very well.  The quality of the sound it recorded was very nice, so gave 5 stars for that 1 component. I am returning these headphones.

title: Works ok, but ear pieces tend to hurt ears in short time.
text: I thought the head set ear pieces would be softer.  When using for a short time the ear pieces start to hurt you ears.  Moving them occasionally helps, but I think they could have been made softer.  The volume control is located about 24 inches from the head set, which is too long.  If you attach the volume control to your shirt there is a loop of cable between the headset and the volume control that can get in the way while writing or other movements of your arms or hands.  The clip on the volume control could be better as well.  It is fixed and can't be clipped on anything other than a thin shirt.  If you want to clip it to a thicker shirt collar you are out of luck.

title: Not worth the money. Already broken
text: This headset worked well at first but has already broken and I'm ordering a different model. You get what you pay for.

title: These headphones freeze up the voice recording software on my ...
text: These headphones freeze up the voice recording software on my Macbook Air. I have conducted a number of tests to confirm it is the headphones. I can use them to listen but purchased them to dictate.

title: No frills headphones that work great
text: As far as USB headphones go, this is a solid buy. Still, I will say that I'm buying them to replace my old pair. They lasted for about three years after being subjected to some admittedly rough usage. The sound quality is good, but nothing to rave about. The microphone though, works tremendously well with skype and other video chat programs. Your voice doesn't get distorted at all. It's especially nice for PC gamers who are tired of crummy mics with lots of feedback. My main reservation is  that, fully extended, they barely fit on my head, and I've got a pretty average sized cranium.

title: Great Headset
text: I really like this headset, especially compared to the set I am replacing. There is no voice delay and they are very clear and comfortable.

title: Logitech Clear Chat
text: So far this headset has been great. I have had it for about a month now and it has held up really well, even though I have dropped it several times. The sound quality is good and no one complains about problem with the mic.

title: Great headset
text: Bought this for my boss who dictates using DRAGON.  He said it is secure, cuts out background noise pretty well.  He liked it so we kept it

title: Good headset with microphone
text: Good sound quality for listening to music or watching video with device, PC. This is for you if you are leery of brain damage or tumors from wireless headsets. Works very well for my listening and VOIP purposes. Good cord length to USB port.

title: Wasn't exactly as easy as plugging it in and using it
text: I have a MacBook Air, so it's about 5 years old, I plugged this into the USB port just fine, but the sound was still coming out of the computer speakers.  This was annoying to have to figure it out, but it didn't take me too long to learn to go into my settings and change the output.  I'm overall just really disappointed with the lack of user instructions in the package.  I had to come to the Amazon product page to get a feel for how things work.  So wasn't as easy as plugging it in and using it.

title: They don't even work
text: Bought this product early June and it has not even been a month. They don't work. This is the 2nd time I purchased them and the 2nd time I've had problems. I will not buy this product/brand again.

title: Wouldn't use any other one
text:
I've been using Dragon NaturallySpeaking to dictate for about 10 years or more. I really don't like the microphone that comes with the Dragon product, so I have experimented with various mics. My favorite by far is the Logitech USB Clear Chat. I love it! It's really comfortable, doesn't slide around and is very accurate.

The only drawback to this microphone is that it dies on me once a year. That's probably because I do extensive dictation every day. I usually buy extended warranties but when I buy it on Amazon, I can't do that. That's my only complaint. Otherwise I'm delighted with this product.

title: Clip breaks way too easily. It has no give.
text: [[VIDEOID:9a9d1fb62efbfdd6306ab029aff65311]] Just opened up package to use for the first time. Now past return window. I'm now forced to use a binder clip to hold this to my blouse. It has no give to it. To take off when you have to go to the bathroom. Invent something with a spring on it . I'll be pissed if my blouse tears.

title: Sound is great, but when I talk
text: Sound is great, but when I talk, my voice does not come through clearly. Total bummer - I really wanted to like these, but I can't use them.

title: 5 stars
text: Great product.  Comfy enough for all-day work-at-home days.  5 stars- would buy again.

title: Noise canceling
text: Wish I could give this 10 stars for noise cancellation. I WFH in our common area and no one can hear my family or the tv in the background when I’m in meetings

title: Great product for the price
text: Great sound and quality. Easy to use and works right out of the box with no special set up required.

title: It seems to work well. I'm using it with ...
text: It seems to work well.  I'm using it with Rosetta Stone and it's doing the job.  Me gusto, mucho!

title: nice sound, on both sides. Pretty comfortable. Better reliability and less maintenance than wireless
text: nice sound, on both sides. Pretty comfortable. Better reliability and less maintenance than wireless

title: Short duration of wearing only
text: Good for sound, bad for ears. I recommend getting a cushioned earpads with your headset, otherwise you can get another one for longer periods of time. If you don't care, buy this anyway. It's good if you plan on wearing it for like 15 minutes or less.

title: Not great.
text: They are not as nice or as comfortable as the Microsoft lx-6000.  My lx-6000 headset broke (common place), so in a pinch, I bought the Logitech ClearChat at Target.  I use this headset with Microsoft Lync all day, and this headset isn't really compatible with Lync.  Also, many people in my meetings have noted that they can hear me breathing through the mic, and if I move the mic, they can't hear me talk at all.  Also, the volume control on the headset actually controls the volume of the computer.  So to get the headset to an audible level that I can actually hear, I had to crank the volume up on the computer itself, so now all alerts and such are blaring loud.  It drives me crazy.  I personally wouldn't recommend this headset at all.

title: New One Every 6 Months!!
text: Avoid these like the plague. Unless you're ready to buy a new one every 6 months, either pay more money, get a different brand, use only once a week, use soup cans tied with string, etc. You get the idea. I've had two sets so far and am on my third. Why keep buying them? I have TMJ, and the adjustable headset is the only one I've found that doesn't give me a headache a minute after I put them on. What's wrong with them? First, after about 3 months the left ear/speaker starts going in and out. I've read this on other sites as well, so I know I'm not the only one with a problem. After about 6 months the wires above the mute button internally separate to the point that you spend minutes trying to get them held in the exact position, and then the least movement cuts you off again. This is embarassing during business calls, or all calls for that matter. The first time it happens, buy a new one then. The problem is only going to get worse. So, yes, I'm the idiot who keeps buying them, but on

title: Good for Skype or Google Video Chat ("Hangouts")
text:
This is an excellent headset for video conferencing. I bought three of them to use with family. I disagree with the reviews that suggest the headset is uncomfortable or not durable. All three people using the sets I bought find the headset comfortable, with clear sound, and the leatherette ear pads seem like they'll hold-up nicely over time.

The unit is truly plug-and-play. No installation of software or drivers needed. However, the only complaint I have is that there's a delay when you plug it in while the computer gets ready. Seems like that delay occurs each time (there is no permanent install so the computer needs to recognize when the USB plug is plugged in each time). It's a little frustrating if you want to use the headset quickly. I'd say the delay is about a minute. That doesn't seem like a long time, but it's an eternity when someone is calling you unexpectedly for a video or audio chat. So, I guess I can leave the unit plugged in all the time, or plan better when

title: These are good headsets, the volume is clear and as long ...
text: I bought this one to replace another one that my daughter broke. These are good headsets, the volume is clear and as long as you talk into the mic speech comes through reasonably well.

title: Amazing crystal clear sound; comfortable for a size 7 1/4 size head
text: I searched and read reviews for 2 days before I decided to take my chance and purchase a Logitech ClearChat H390.  I have used Logitech products with my work previously and I knew that Logitech has reliable products are reliable but I wasn't sure if they were the best (because my job tended to use lower grade equipment) until I made my personal purchase of this product.  I am often one who believes if you pay more you get better quality.  However, not so in this case!  I had compared the prices of a few other brands and it was in the back of my mind that I should spend more since I plan to use this for online class and seminar participation.  For the price, this headset had superb performance - crisp clear microphone (everyone in the seminar could hear me clearly) and I could hear the content from other presenters also very clearly.  I saw the comments about discomfort but I did not experience that during participation in about 3 two hour seminars so far.  My head is a size 7 1/2 and t

title: Very clear
text: I own three headsets wory various connectors. This is by far my favorite. The sound is clear, the microphone is good, and the mute button is great.

title: I was able to get it to do what I needed.
text: I bought this to use with my Rosetta Stone software.  I am using this on a 6 year old Mac Book Pro as the headset that came with the original software stopped working.  The micrphone works on this Logitech ClearChat Headset, but I could never get the ear or listening piece to work.  I adjusted it in the computer settings etc..,  and it just would not pick it up so I use the microphone from this headset and my own ear buds plugged into another port and I can do what I need to do... Obviously not the best situation, but it is a pretty old computer so in all honesty it could be the reason I could not get the headset to work properly... but with my jerry rigging I can carry on.

title: Not the same quality as before
text: I used these headphones a while back and was awesome for even gaming. Not sure what happened but the sound quality definitely took a hit. It's no longer very clear and not even the Mic and people's voices are very clear which was the perk of this headset before. Overall, this decent headset became a cheap headset that lost all the quality it had before. Disappointed.

title: very good.
text: I use this for my phone calls with magic jack.

title: Very good headset
text: Purchased this as a replacement for a set that began to malfunction.  So far it has proven functional and comfortable for me.  Coupled with what I consider was a reasonable price, I believe it is a very good value compared to some of the more expensive devices available.  It is worth a look.

title: Works fine
text: I bought one of these because we use the same one at work. With the pandemic, they have us in the office every other day, and working from home on the other days). I bought this so I don't have to carry my headset back and forth every day, now I have one for in-office and one for home-office. Works fine, USB connected to the computer, has an in-line mute and volume button, the microphone swivels up and down, and the headset is padded and adjustable to your head. The ear pieces are ON EAR, not over the ear, but they're decently padded. I don't use mine a ton, so I can't attest to how comfortable they are after hours of wear, but they work fine for when I occasionally have to talk on the phone to someone.

title: After 8 years as a happy customer, I cannot buy this model again.
text: I use a headset anywhere from 60% - 90% of my day. My co-workers frequently have problems with their wireless headsets that cost in the hundreds when switching between meeting platforms or other issues, but I never had problems with this headset model until recently. I found over the years that I had to replace them every few years but they are so inexpensive and were 100% reliable in all situations that I was okay with that. I just purchased a new pair and plugged it into the same usb port and according to the folks I talked to, the sound cut out about 50% of the time. Nothing I did in adjusting settings or plugging into another usb port solved the problem. I definitely don't have time for this and will not be purchasing this model again.

title: q
text: These are great headphones for what I am doing; learning French online. They are comfortable and light weight. The microphone picks up quite well and is not flimsy. Not sure you could do better for this price point.[[ASIN:B000UXZQ42 Logitech ClearChat Comfort/USB Headset H390, Noise Cancelling Microphone, Headphones for Computer (Black)]]

title: Great basic/entry-level conferencing headset
text:
I work remotely occasionally, and picked up this headset after some coworkers complained about the sound from the built-in microphone in my laptop.

My initial impression was that the headphones felt kind of cheap. Marginally better than the earphones you'd get with a tape player in the 90's. Although they feel cheap, they're also really light. So after wearing them for a few minutes, I stopped noticing I was wearing them. The sound through the headphones was pretty good, and a remote coworker said I sounded great on a video call the first time he heard the microphone.

So if you're looking for a basic conferencing headset with pretty good audio quality, I think these hit the mark. And although they seem a little cheap, they're also so light that they're pretty comfortable.

title: Defective
text: The usb connector was defective.  I had to return the headset.

title: Work Great
text: I work from home and use these for conference calls. So far they have worked very well. Great value for the price.

title: Five Stars
text: Good clear sound works great with my Rosetta stone software

title: LOVe this - but it is  NOT a beginner book
text: Be warned - this is the third in a series of three books, and unless you have worked through the earlier two you will not get the real value.  Kum Nye Tibetan Yoga is the starter - it's not nearly as user-friendly but it IS important to the learning process and will take you 6-12 months of diligent work to assimilate.  Then Joy of Being - another year.  THEN this book.  Highly recommend the YouTube videos by Arnaud Maitland to help make sense of it all.  It's work, but is very worth while.

title: Good headset.  Lousy ear pads.
text: This headset works well for the price, and is able to work on my corporate laptop which is locked down. However the ear pads are very fragile and will begin to peel and flake, leaving black dust and pieces all over the desk and the side of your head.

title: Fantastic Buy
text: They bought these for us at work. They work so well, thar I bought a pair for home when I telework. They dramatically improve the ability to hear in meetings when using zoom or another software.  I would definitely recommend these headphones for both professional and private use.

title: Sounds is good.
text: I am very happy with the sound and overall fit.  However,  the headphones start to hurt on long calls when wearing glasses.  Overall it is s great product.

title: Stops working after a year
text:
I bought these headphones because I thought they would last me a long time and the reviews were great.
The first pair I got at Walmart and those lasted me 2 years and then they stopped working and the latest one I bought on Amazon and thet just lasted me 1 year. I am so disappointed with these. I won't be buying them again.

title: Possible fake!
text: Felt like cheap plastic. Came with no original box just a plastic bag, I don’t know if maybe just Logitech sold it in bulk to the seller this way as it was easier for shipping and all for them. However, made me somewhat suspicious it could be a fake as it felt very cheap and other reviewers mentioned the same thing. I tried using the mute button on a face time call with my computer and after I clicked it the audio was lost completely, I tried messing with the audio settings on my computer and not even that would fix it. No other headphones I have ever used have done this and I have used headsets to work on a computer for 10 years. The sides of the headphones just said logi on them. I returned this!

title: One Star
text: Doesn't block noise. Not over the ear earphones.

title: Fairly good headset
text:
I wear a headset for the better part of a day. I like to use headsets rather than the phone handset because it keeps my hands free.

These definitely do the job with noise cancelling and the volume control works very well. I've been told once or twice that people have trouble hearing me at times, but since I don't know if that's the headset or their phones, I deducted a single star.

My only complaint is the clip that puts the volume control and mute button together is very difficult to hook onto anything other than very thin fabric. I have to struggle to get it on and off my jacket during the day. My suggestion to the manufacturer, make the clip easier to slide fabric into or make it a clip that opens.

title: Works, but uncomfortable and not very adjustable
text: I can wear these for about 45 minutes. After that my ears hurt and there's not alot of options to adjust these.

title: Great headphones
text: I use this to do my Rosetta Stone lessons and it is working well.  My biggest problem is that I sometimes forget that I'm attached and walk away from the computer while still wearing the headset.  LOL!  The only complaint I have is small.  They are a little tight, so they can get uncomfortable when wearing them for extended periods.  By the time the pressure gets to me I'm usually ready to quit studying German anyway.  Great sound in and out!

title: Nice Headset, works with older computers
text:
My AIO (HP) is ancient (Windows 8). The fancy Beats sound system stopped being properly supported by MS Windows 10 a long time ago. This headset takes over and works properly for conferencing and teaching and I don't have to use the stupid college-supplied iPad. And for a decent price.

We'll see if it's durable.

title: Clear and great price
text: Works great. Only problem for me. Very tight against my head/ears. I am not sure if it is not suppose to plug in all the way or not. It doesn't on my laptop. Very clear and does come with a V E R Y long cord. Don't know why it is that long. Gets in my way.

title: Two Stars
text: Its not very comfortable and it broke so I had to tape it together.

title: Great sound, iffy fit (for my head/ears)
text:
The sound/mic quality is extremely clear and clean, so no complaints there.
Two issues - The set is somewhat bulky and does not fold, thus taking up a lot of space in my laptop bag. The other issue is that it sits a bit tight on the ears, making it uncomfortable after a couple of hours. It's a little easier on the ears if I move the cups slightly forward, but then that lets external sounds in. That said, it could be the shape of my head, and you might not have this issue.

title: Heavy on my head. Otherwise ok.
text:
Ive had this headphones set for over a year now and it does the job, BUT it is too heavy. Any day when I have a lot of meetings and use it for extended periods of time, the top of my skull hurts. Literally hurts. I noticed it in the beginning but decided to keep it and give it a try. Well, I still use it every day.
I would invest in a cordless set if I were to buy it now, because this long cord is also a pain.
Overall will do the job.

title: Five Stars
text: Works great as expected. No complaints

title: Wife likes
text: I bought several similar head sets for wife to work remotely. This is the one she uses over the others. That speaks volumes.

title: Replaced 10 year old headset from same brand
text: With distance learning we have been using lots of headsets. My 11 year old prefers this style.  He was using a very well loved abs much used headset from this brand that was 10 years old, so we decided to replace it with something from the same brand.

title: Fits my small ears/head
text: I had the previous generation of this headset so I knew what I was getting. When I get glasses/hats I have to buy adolescent sizes (preteen-teen), so this headset fits me well at the smallest adjustment. I can still hear people if I have no sound or low sound while wearing which is what I prefer. The sound quality is satisfactory.  There are volume controls on the side that easily can be pressed against my chair... so I have to be aware of it and maybe use the clip that is on the back of it (it slips over clothing doesn't actually 'clip'). the cord is longer I believe than the other generation. Aside from the logo and the sound perhaps being a bit better it is essentially like the previous version I had purchased.

title: Not clear sound
text: I brought this last month now when i use this during my work meeting most of the time facing issue like robotic sound now i see review good on this product and brought now not working. I will not recommended this product

title: Great for PC Conferences
text: perfect for the conferences via PC.  Quite comfortable and adjustable volume / mute button easy to use

title: Good value and quality
text: I am very pleased with the sound quality of these headphones. They have helped immensely with my working from home and with meetings

title: Using with WebEx
text: Works fine with WebEx. No problem so far.

title: Too cheap
text:
My headset came with the mic on the right side, not the left.
The left speaker is louder than the right one. It's not my hearing nor the computer, just poor quality.

title: Great Headset, using it for Ham Radio Remote, works very well....
text: excellent headset .. used for Ham Radio...

title: Good inexpensive headset
text: It's comfortable, works well, and easy to use. I have recommended to family and would buy again.

title: so far so good
text: I plugged it in, it took the computer just a moment to download the driver, and it works like a charm. I'll post an update if I ever have any problems with it.

title: High Quality Headset
text: The vinyl covering can start coming off from the oil of your skin and hair but other than that, it's a great product. A lot of similar products have the same issue.

title: good for recording
text: I needed a good mic for recording presentations that would be cut up and used over video. I found that you need to prime it before you start talking, so if I just went right into your script there would be feedback I couldn't easily crop out, so saying 'test' or something to get the mic ready and then starting the script stopped that fuzzy noise and i could easily crop out the 'test' words.

title: Great headphones for the price
text: Great headphones for the price. No technical issues encountered with these at all, the only reason we replace them is because my husband and I are a pair of klutz and run them over occasionally with our chairs.

title: so far so good
text: Not the based sound quality but it is comfortable.

title: Excellent headset for a great price!
text: The sound quality is awesome for the headphones. The mic quality isn't quite as good but it serves it's purpose well for talking and chatting. It's very comfortable and can be adjusted to fit a pretty large head if need be. I didn't adjust mine at all and it fits perfectly.  It has pretty good noise cancellation, and it's easy and practical to use! It's wonderful for the low price!

title: Logitech
text: These have always been my goto headset for skype and other online chats. There only real downfall is the ear pads rot and replacements are not offered

title: Also the headset is not really comfortable at all
text: This is not noise cancelling. People can still hear background noise so I'm surprised it's being marketing as "noise canceling". Also the headset is not really comfortable at all. I can only wear it for an hour before I get a headache from my head being squeezed.

title: Not Recommended.
text: I have to say, this is the most painful headset I've ever had the displeasure of using. I would have returned it, but I convinced myself that perhaps it would get better with use. But no, here it is three months later, and the thing still gives me a horrible headache within about fifteen minutes of putting it on. I have a fairly small head, so it's not as though the thing had to try and stretch across a wide space. No, it's just made so that it crushes the ears against the head.

title: Great Purchase
text: Excellent purchase for conference calls.

title: Noise is cancelled
text: These headphones serve their purpose but the volume is not easy to adjust and they tend to slide on my head.

title: SAFE BUY..... (IMO)
text: I use this Head Set mainly for Dictating and SPEAKING (Writing) a book using the software "Dragon Naturally Speaking"... They do a great job of deadening background noise and the Mic at least with Dragon Naturally Speaking makes my speech about 96% understandable......

title: Great value
text: This was my fourth pair that I purchased. They usually last me 6 to 9 months because I travel so much and they simply get stepped on by me or I’ll break them in some other way. They’re not breaking because they’re bad material they’re breaking because I’m a klutz. I’m at this price I don’t worry about it and when something goes wrong just order a new one but the microphone and earphones are great I hope I don’t have to buy a fifth one for at least six months :-)

title: Great for sound but poor for communication
text: Sorry I threw away the packaging.  I use this for VOIP phone conversations.  The sound quality is really good.  Unfortunately, I need to be able to have a conversation using this and it is really spotty.  If you are just listening to music, videos, etc, this is fine.  If you need to use it as a communication device, I would skip it.

title: Works as described
text: Headphones used for work Zoom calls.  Audio is good.

title: Works well for me
text: These were recommended by a friend and they work well for me.

title: One Star
text:
Does not work with Ventrilo.

Edit I gave this one star because ventrilo was the only reason I purchased this. It is of no use to me if it doesn't work with the program I bought it for. I could not even test out the mic or anything since it doesn't work with my speaking software. If you are not using ventrilo maybe if will be a better rating for you but I wasted my time and had to return it.

title: Very comfortable but there's a hum
text:
I actually used one of these for years, until it finally started to misbehave - people on the other end of a Skype call said that they were only getting the middle of words, not the beginning or end, and no amount of tweaking with settings made any difference. Having been pleased with the comfort of the ear pads and the headset quality itself for so long, I ordered another.

But the new one had a very noticeable hum. Since I do a lot of call recording as I conduct interviews for clients (with the interviewee's knowledge, of course), call quality is important to me - not to mention the irritation that the hum would cause at the other end. Some research online revealed that this has been a problem with this headset for some time, and Logitech has apparently not addressed it. I'm surprised, frankly; I have been very pleased with other Logitech products. And I don't know why the problem showed up in the newer version but not in the older version.

I decided to keep this

title: Product works fine. I have used this model before
text: Product works fine.  I have used this model before.  I needed a second pair.  It was odd that they arrived in a plastic bag in a box.

title: Nice Quality
text: Nice quality headset. Will buy again.

title: replacement headphones
text: I got these as a replacement pair of headphones for my ones I use to teach online and they work much better. The volume in the headphones is clearer and easy to turn up or down. In addition, they were easy to install and begin using immediately when I received my order. I would recommend these to anyone gaming or teaching online! The microphone length is perfect and they are comfortable to wear for long periods of time.

title: Work well and are comfortable
text: Have been using these almost daily for about 10 months. They work well and are comfortable.

title: Good Headset
text: I am happy with the quality and usefulness of this product.

title: Work very well with webinars and on-line conferences
text: Headsets really help with on-line listening and speaking quality. My previous headset, a different brand, the ear pads were falling apart while the electronics were fine - but the ear pads were the weakest element - so I tossed them away.  My new logitechs work great. Webinars, GoToMeetings, presentations - I get good marks on the quality of my audio. and I like the mute button I can hold in my hand and the little red light on it easily tells me I am or am not muted.  I have had them about 2 months now.

title: Was cracked at the volume knob when in got it
text: Not sure if it was because of the product or the delivery person...stopped working in less than a month
